text: No correlation was found between running mileage or gender and sustaining a lower extremity injury in youth distance runners.
Middle school runners report fewer lower extremity injuries than high school runners in distance running.
population: middle school and high school runners

text: Cross country running accounts for less than 10% of injuries in youth under 15 compared to other track and field activities.
Youth can participate in distance running with minimal adverse musculoskeletal effects on the lower extremities.
Youth distance runners may have increased vulnerability to growth plate (apophyseal) injuries, including hip fractures.
Research on the effects of distance running in children under 10 years old is essentially nonexistent, leaving safety recommendations for this age group unsupported by evidence.
